refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on achieving a specific teleoperation link availability. The phrase 'customer-defined' keeps it solution-neutral by not specifying how availability is achieved, only that the target must be met. The FR does not contain multiple obligations or 'and/or' conditions, so no splitting is needed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or exceeds the customer-defined target (e.g., ≥ 0.999).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication channels to ensure failover capability
Use adaptive link management to dynamically allocate resources and maintain availability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring with automatic recovery mechanisms
Design the teleoperation link with modular components to isolate failures and enable quick repair or replacement
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication channels to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy reduces dependency on a single communication path, minimizing coupling between failure modes
coupling_check.notes: Redundant channels provide independence between communication paths, supporting high availability without tightly coupling system components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundant channels can be implemented within operational policies and safety plans
Risk that redundancy may increase system complexity and cost
Assumes evaluation period and measurement methods are well-defined and consistent
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the evaluation period and measurement methodology for link availability to ensure clarity in acceptance criteria.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall communication and network management system responsible for implementing redundant communication channels to ensure failover capability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connections used for communication redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health data over redundant channels to external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as redundant communication channels are core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as failover logic is centralized here.
Partial containment possible but would require diffusion of communication logic into vehicle systems, increasing coupling.
Teleoperation depends on communication but implementing redundancy here would cause significant diffusion and coupling.
Safety Services may use communication channels but implementing redundancy here would be indirect and cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant communication channels, as it directly manages network communication and failover mechanisms, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If failover requires coordination with Safety Services or Teleoperation, interfaces must be well defined to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant communication channels directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target by providing failover capability, which is essential to meet the availability acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level vehicle hardware interfaces may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
Coordination with Safety Services or Teleoperation modules could introduce diffusion if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk as it centralizes the failover logic and redundant communication channels. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the containment boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the link availability (≥ 0.999 or customer-defined target) over the agreed evaluation period through the NGHE Solution module's telemetry outputs and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, with well-defined external interfaces for network link and telemetry, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Define clear and robust interfaces between NGHE Solution and dependent modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to minimize diffusion and coupling risks.
Document coordination mechanisms for failover with Safety Services and Teleoperation to ensure architectural clarity and maintainability.
Include explicit verification procedures in the NGHE Solution module to measure link availability against the acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and acceptable diffusion risks. The NGHE Solution module is well-contained with high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are established. Recommended revisions focus on interface definitions and documentation rather than fundamental decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize the current level with these refinements rather than further decomposing, which could introduce complexity and diffusion risks.

revise.why: To define clear and robust interfaces between NGHE Solution and dependent modules, document coordination mechanisms for failover, and include explicit verification procedures to ensure architectural clarity, maintainability, and verification feasibility.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module sufficiently encapsulates the failover logic and redundant communication channels.
Verification of link availability through telemetry is reliable and feasible.
Interfaces with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules can be clearly defined and maintained.
open_risks: Potential coupling with lower-level vehicle hardware interfaces.
Coordination complexity with Safety Services and Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not well defined.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface definitions and coordination mechanisms.
Verification procedure details and telemetry data supporting link availability measurement.
